title: 10 best herbal teas for viral hepatitis
intro_desc: Herbal teas for viral hepatitis are infusions made from plants and herbs that can help alleviate symptoms and support liver function during viral hepatitis. These teas often contain anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiviral properties that can help reduce inflammation, protect liver cells, and boost the immune system. Examples of herbal teas that can be beneficial for viral hepatitis include milk thistle, dandelion, licorice root, and turmeric. For instance, milk thistle is well-known for its ability to protect the liver and promote liver cell regeneration, while dandelion and licorice root can help improve liver function and reduce inflammation. By incorporating these herbal teas into their daily routine, individuals with viral hepatitis can experience improved liver health, reduced symptoms, and an overall better quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tea helps with viral hepatitis because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce liver inflammation and oxidative stress, which are common in viral hepatitis. Dandelion tea is also known to improve liver function by increasing bile production and promoting the flow of bile from the liver to the gallbladder, which can help remove toxins from the body. Additionally, dandelion tea contains compounds that may have antiviral properties, which could potentially help inhibit the replication of the hepatitis virus in the body. However, it is important to note that while dandelion tea may be beneficial for individuals with viral hepatitis, it should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment and should be used in consultation with a healthcare provider.
remedy_properties: Antihepatotoxic: Dandelion tea helps to protect the liver from damage and supports its function, which is important in viral hepatitis as it can help to reduce the burden on the liver. The active constituents responsible for this property include taraxacin, taraxacerin, and cichoric acid.

Antioxidant: Dandelion tea contains high levels of antioxidants, such as vitamin C and luteolin, which can help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the liver, important in viral hepatitis as it can help to reduce liver damage.

Antiviral: Dandelion tea has been shown to have antiviral properties, which can help to inhibit the replication of the hepatitis virus. The active constituents responsible for this property include chicoric acid and cichotycin.

Hepatoprotective: Dandelion tea has been shown to have hepatoprotective properties, which can help to protect the liver from damage and promote its regeneration. The active constituents responsible for this property include taraxasterol and taraxacin.

Diuretic: Dandelion tea acts as a diuretic, which can help to eliminate toxins from the body and reduce the workload on the liver. The active constituents responsible for this property include potassium and tannins.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain bioactive compounds that exhibit antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties, beneficial for treating viral hepatitis.
Leaves: Rich in antioxidants that help protect the liver from oxidative stress and support liver function during viral hepatitis.
Flowers: High in flavonoids, which have been shown to have hepatoprotective effects, aiding in the management of viral hepatitis.
Buds: Packed with vitamins and minerals that support the immune system and overall liver health during viral hepatitis.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 teaspoons of fresh dandelion flowers or 1-2 teaspoons of dried dandelion leaves.
Rinse the dandelion parts thoroughly to remove any dirt or impurities.
Coarsely chop the dandelion flowers or leaves and place them in a tea infuser or teapot.
Pour 8 ounces of boiling water over the dandelion parts and let them steep for 5-10 minutes.
Remove the tea infuser or strain the tea, then drink it while it's still warm, up to three times a day for viral hepatitis treatment.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric tea helps with viral hepatitis because it contains a powerful compound called curcumin, which has been shown to have antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties. These properties can help to reduce inflammation in the liver, which is often damaged by the hepatitis virus. Additionally, curcumin can help to stimulate the production of bile, which is necessary for digestion and can support liver function. Furthermore, studies have suggested that curcumin may help to inhibit the replication of the hepatitis virus, which can help to slow down the progression of the disease. Therefore, drinking turmeric tea can be a beneficial addition to a treatment plan for viral hepatitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Turmeric tea's anti-inflammatory property, due to the presence of curcumin, can help reduce liver inflammation caused by viral hepatitis.
Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content in turmeric tea, primarily from curcumin, helps protect liver cells from oxidative stress and damage caused by viral hepatitis.
Antiviral: Turmeric tea has been shown to have antiviral properties, potentially inhibiting the replication of viral hepatitis, thanks to curcumin and other active constituents.
Hepatoprotective: Turmeric tea's hepatoprotective property, attributed to curcumin and other compounds, helps support liver health and function during viral hepatitis.
Immune-boosting: Turmeric tea can help strengthen the immune system, allowing the body to better fight viral hepatitis, due to its content of curcumin and other immune-enhancing compounds.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: The rhizomes of the turmeric tea plant are used to make medicinal tea for viral hepatitis due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce liver inflammation and protect liver cells.

Leaves: The leaves of the turmeric tea plant are used in medicinal tea for viral hepatitis because they contain curcumin, a compound with antiviral properties that can help inhibit the replication of hepatitis viruses.
Flowers: The flowers of the turmeric tea plant are used in medicinal tea for viral hepatitis due to their hepatoprotective properties, which can help prevent liver damage and promote liver health.

Seeds: The seeds of the turmeric tea plant are used in medicinal tea for viral hepatitis because they contain curcumin and other compounds with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce liver inflammation and protect liver cells.
remedy_recipe: In a saucepan, combine 2 cups of water, 1 teaspoon of grated fresh turmeric root (or 1/2 teaspoon of dried turmeric powder), and 1/2 teaspoon of crushed black peppercorns.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the solids.
Add honey or lemon juice to taste, if desired.
Drink the tea while it's still warm, up to three times a day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tea helps with viral hepatitis because it contains active compounds like glycyrrhizin, which has been shown to have antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties. These properties can help reduce inflammation in the liver, one of the primary symptoms of viral hepatitis. Additionally, glycyrrhizin can inhibit the replication of the hepatitis virus, potentially slowing down the progression of the disease. Furthermore, licorice tea can help improve liver function by promoting the production of bile, which is essential for digestion and eliminating toxins from the body. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare professional before consuming licorice tea for hepatitis, as excessive consumption can lead to side effects such as high blood pressure and potassium depletion.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Licorice tea's anti-inflammatory properties, due to its active constituents like glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, can help alleviate liver inflammation caused by viral hepatitis.
Antiviral: Licorice tea has been shown to exhibit antiviral properties, particularly against hepatitis C virus, due to the presence of compounds such as glycyrrhizin and isoliquiritigenin.
Hepatoprotective: Licorice tea's hepatoprotective properties can help protect liver cells from damage caused by viral hepatitis, thanks to the active constituents like glycyrrhizin and liquiritin.

Immune-boosting: Licorice tea's ability to boost the immune system, attributed to the presence of active compounds like glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, can aid in the body's fight against viral hepatitis.
Antioxidant: Licorice tea contains potent antioxidants, such as liquiritin and isoliquiritigenin, which can help reduce oxidative stress in the liver caused by viral hepatitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice tea plant contain triterpenoid saponins, which have been shown to have antiviral properties that may help in treating viral hepatitis.
Leaves: The leaves of the licorice tea plant contain flavonoids and coumarins, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce liver damage caused by viral hepatitis.
Flowers: The flowers of the licorice tea plant contain glycosides and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have hepatoprotective effects, making them useful in treating viral hepatitis.
Seeds: The seeds of the licorice tea plant contain essential oils and polysaccharides, which have been shown to have immunomodulatory effects, making them useful in treating viral hepatitis.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 cup of water and 1 tablespoon of dried licorice root, then bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce heat, let it simmer for 10-15 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the used licorice root.
Add honey or lemon to taste, but avoid milk or other dairy products.
Drink the tea while warm, 2-3 times a day, as part of a holistic approach to managing viral hepatitis.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milk thistle tea helps with viral hepatitis because it contains silymarin, a flavonoid that has been shown to have antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties. These properties can help to protect the liver from damage caused by the hepatitis virus, reduce inflammation, and support the liver's natural healing process. Additionally, milk thistle tea may help to improve liver function by increasing the production of bile, which is needed for digestion and the elimination of toxins from the body. The antioxidant properties of milk thistle tea may also help to protect liver cells from oxidative stress, which can further support liver health and function. However, it is important to note that while milk thistle tea may be beneficial for individuals with viral hepatitis, it should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment or advice from a healthcare professional.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Milk thistle tea can reduce inflammation in the liver, which is important for viral hepatitis as inflammation can exacerbate liver damage caused by the virus. This property is attributed to a flavonoid compound called silymarin.

Antioxidant: Milk thistle tea has antioxidant properties that can help protect liver cells from damage caused by free radicals, which are increased in viral hepatitis. Silymarin is also responsible for this antioxidant effect.

Hepatoprotective: Milk thistle tea can help protect the liver from damage and promote liver cell regeneration, which is crucial in viral hepatitis as the virus can cause significant liver cell damage. The hepatoprotective effect is primarily due to silymarin and its active constituents.

Immune-modulating: Milk thistle tea can help regulate the immune response, which is important in viral hepatitis as an overactive immune response can contribute to liver damage. The immune-modulating effect of milk thistle is attributed to silymarin and its ability to influence cytokine production.

Antiviral: Some studies suggest that milk thistle tea may have antiviral properties, which could potentially help inhibit the replication of the hepatitis virus. The antiviral effect is thought to be due to the silymarin compound, although more research is needed to confirm this.
remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds of the milk thistle plant contain silymarin, a flavonoid that has been shown to have liver-protecting properties, making it useful for treating viral hepatitis.

Leaves: The leaves of the milk thistle plant are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which can help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the liver, making it beneficial for those with viral hepatitis.

Flowers: The flowers of the milk thistle plant contain flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that have been shown to have hepatoprotective effects, making it a useful addition to medicinal tea for viral hepatitis.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the milk thistle plant contain high levels of silymarin, which has been shown to have antiviral properties and can help to prevent the replication of the hepatitis virus.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 1-2 teaspoons of dried milk thistle seeds and place them in a tea infuser or teapot.
Boil 8 ounces of water and pour it over the milk thistle seeds.
Allow the tea to steep for 10-15 minutes to extract the beneficial compounds.
Strain the tea to remove the seeds and discard them.
Drink the milk thistle tea while it is still warm, up to three times a day for viral hepatitis treatment.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Skullcap tea helps with viral hepatitis because it contains anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that can potentially reduce liver inflammation and combat the hepatitis virus. The herb's bioactive compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, have been shown to protect liver cells from damage and support liver function. Skullcap tea may also help improve the immune system, which is crucial for fighting off viral infections. However, it is important to note that while skullcap tea may provide some benefits, it should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment for viral hepatitis. Always consult with a healthcare provider for proper diagnosis and treatment.
remedy_properties: Antiviral activity: Skullcap tea contains compounds such as scutellarin and baicalin, which have been shown to inhibit the replication of hepatitis B and C viruses, thus helping to reduce the viral load in the body.

Anti-inflammatory effects: The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in skullcap tea can help reduce inflammation in the liver, alleviating symptoms and potentially slowing the progression of viral hepatitis.
Hepatoprotective properties: Skullcap tea has been found to protect liver cells from damage caused by oxidative stress and toxins, promoting liver health and function during viral hepatitis.

Immune system support: The antioxidants and polysaccharides found in skullcap tea can help boost the immune system, enabling the body to better fight off viral infections and promote recovery from hepatitis.

Antioxidant activity: Skullcap tea contains high levels of antioxidants, which can help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress in the liver, protecting it from damage caused by viral hepatitis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain high levels of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which can help reduce liver inflammation caused by viral hepatitis.
Flowers: Rich in flavonoids and other polyphenols, which have been shown to have antiviral properties and may help inhibit the replication of hepatitis viruses.
Stems: Contain tannins and other compounds that have been shown to have hepatoprotective effects, helping to protect liver cells from damage caused by viral hepatitis.
Roots: Have been used in traditional medicine to treat liver diseases, and contain compounds that may help reduce liver inflammation and promote liver health.
Seeds: Contain essential oils and other compounds that have been shown to have antiviral and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help reduce liver damage caused by hepatitis viruses.
remedy_recipe: In a mortar, crush 2 tablespoons of dried skullcap herb (Scutellaria lateriflora) to release its active compounds.
Place the crushed herb in a tea infuser and put it in a cup or teapot.
Pour boiling water (250ml) over the infuser and let it steep for 10-15 minutes to allow the extraction of the medicinal constituents.
Remove the infuser and discard the spent herb.
Drink the tea, preferably 2-3 times a day, between meals, for a week or two, to help alleviate viral hepatitis symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tea helps with viral hepatitis because it contains antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties that may aid in reducing inflammation and combating the virus. The tea is made from the leaves of the Melissa officinalis plant, which have been used in traditional medicine for centuries. Studies suggest that lemon balm may have a protective effect on the liver, potentially reducing oxidative stress and supporting liver function. Additionally, lemon balm tea has been shown to promote relaxation and improve mood, which can help alleviate some of the stress and anxiety often associated with a viral hepatitis diagnosis. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare provider before incorporating lemon balm tea or any other herbal remedies into a treatment plan for viral hepatitis.
remedy_properties: Antiviral property: Lemon balm tea contains rosmarinic acid, which has been shown to have antiviral effects and may help to inhibit the replication of the hepatitis virus.
Anti-inflammatory property: Lemon balm tea contains eugenol, which has anti-inflammatory effects and may help to reduce inflammation in the liver caused by viral hepatitis.
Hepatoprotective property: Lemon balm tea contains flavonoids, which have been shown to have hepatoprotective effects and may help to protect the liver from damage caused by viral hepatitis.
Antioxidant property: Lemon balm tea contains rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid, which have antioxidant effects and may help to protect liver cells from oxidative stress caused by viral hepatitis.

Immune-boosting property: Lemon balm tea contains various antioxidants, such as caffeic acid and rosmarinic acid, that can help to boost the immune system and may help the body to fight off viral hepatitis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of lemon balm tea plant are rich in antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in treating viral hepatitis.
Flowers: The flowers of lemon balm tea plant contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have hepatoprotective effects, making them useful in treating viral hepatitis.
Stems: The stems of lemon balm tea plant contain rosmarinic acid, which has been shown to have antiviral and anti-inflammatory effects, making them beneficial in treating viral hepatitis.
Seeds: The seeds of lemon balm tea plant contain essential oils and polyphenols, which have been shown to have hepatoprotective effects, making them useful in treating viral hepatitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of fresh lemon balm leaves or use 1 tablespoon of dried leaves and rinse them thoroughly.
Place the leaves in a teapot and pour 8 ounces of boiling water over them, allowing the tea to steep for 5-10 minutes.
Add honey or lemon juice to taste, if desired.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the leaves.
Drink the tea while it's still warm, 2-3 times a day for best results.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Andrographis tea helps with viral hepatitis because it contains bioactive compounds, particularly andrographolide, which has been shown to have antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties. These properties may help reduce viral replication and alleviate inflammation in the liver, thereby potentially slowing down the progression of viral hepatitis. Additionally, andrographis has been found to protect liver cells from damage and support liver function, making it a potentially beneficial herbal remedy for individuals with viral hepatitis. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new supplement regimen.
remedy_properties: Antiviral activity: Andrographis tea has been shown to have antiviral properties, which can help to prevent the replication of the hepatitis virus in the body. This is important for viral hepatitis because the virus can cause inflammation and damage to the liver. The active constituents that give andrographis its antiviral properties include diterpene lactones, such as andrographolide.

Anti-inflammatory activity: Andrographis tea has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation in the liver caused by the hepatitis virus. This is important for viral hepatitis because inflammation can lead to liver damage and scarring. The active constituents that give andrographis its anti-inflammatory properties include diterpene lactones, such as andrographolide.

Immune-boosting activity: Andrographis tea has been shown to boost the immune system, which can help the body to better fight off the hepatitis virus. This is important for viral hepatitis because a strong immune system is better able to fight off the virus and prevent it from causing further damage to the liver. The active constituents that give andrographis its immune-boosting properties include diterpene lactones, such as andrographolide.

Antioxidant activity: Andrographis tea has antioxidant properties, which can help to protect liver cells from damage caused by free radicals. This is important for viral hepatitis because the virus can cause an increase in free radical production, which can lead to liver damage. The active constituents that give andrographis its antioxidant properties include flavonoids and phenolic acids.

Hepatoprotective activity: Andrographis tea has been shown to have hepatoprotective properties, which means that it can help to protect the liver from damage caused by the hepatitis virus. This is important for viral hepatitis because the virus can cause inflammation and damage to the liver. The active constituents that give andrographis its hepatoprotective properties include diterpene lactones, such as andrographolide.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain bioactive compounds that have been shown to have antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in treating viral hepatitis.
Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, the leaves of andrographis tea plant help in reducing liver inflammation and protecting liver cells.
Flowers: Packed with andrographolide, a compound with antiviral and hepatoprotective properties, making the flowers useful in treating viral hepatitis.
Seeds: Contain high levels of andrographolide, a potent anti-inflammatory and antiviral agent, making the seeds effective in treating viral hepatitis.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 2 cups of water and add 2 teaspoons of dried andrographis leaves, simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Remove the pot from heat, cover and let it steep for an additional 10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the andrographis leaves.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Drink the tea while warm, up to three times a day for viral hepatitis support.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eastern purple coneflower tea helps with viral hepatitis because it contains powerful antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that support liver function and boost the immune system. The herb's active compounds, such as echinacea and cichoric acid, have been shown to have antiviral effects, which can help inhibit the replication of the hepatitis virus. Additionally, the tea can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the liver, promoting healing and recovery. Regular consumption of eastern purple coneflower tea may also help improve liver enzyme levels and overall liver health in individuals with hepatitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Eastern purple coneflower tea can help reduce inflammation in the liver, which is important for viral hepatitis as it can reduce damage to liver cells caused by inflammation. The active constituents responsible for this property include chlorogenic acid and caffeic acid.

Antioxidant: The tea has antioxidant properties that can help protect liver cells from oxidative stress, which is beneficial for viral hepatitis as it can prevent further liver damage. The main antioxidants in eastern purple coneflower tea are phenolic acids and flavonoids.

Antiviral: Eastern purple coneflower tea has been shown to have antiviral properties, which can help inhibit the replication of the hepatitis virus. The active constituents responsible for this property include isoquercitrin and luteolin-7-glucoside.

Immune-boosting: The tea can help strengthen the immune system, which is crucial for individuals with viral hepatitis as a strong immune system can help the body fight off the virus. The immune-boosting properties of eastern purple coneflower tea are attributed to its high content of polysaccharides.

Hepatoprotective: Eastern purple coneflower tea has hepatoprotective properties, which means it can protect the liver from damage. This is particularly important for viral hepatitis as it can help prevent further liver damage caused by the virus. The hepatoprotective effects of the tea are mainly due to its high content of phenolic acids and flavonoids.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants, the leaves help reduce oxidative stress and support liver function.
Flowers: Contain anti-inflammatory properties, helping alleviate symptoms associated with viral hepatitis.
Seeds: High in essential fatty acids, seeds support liver health and enhance the immune system.
Roots: Packed with antiviral compounds, roots help inhibit viral replication and protect liver cells.
remedy_recipe: Measure 2 teaspoons of dried eastern purple coneflower flowers and add to a tea infuser or teapot.
Boil 1 cup of water and let it cool down for a few minutes until it reaches a temperature of around 200°F (93°C).
Pour the hot water over the coneflower flowers and let it steep for 5-10 minutes to extract the beneficial compounds.
Remove the tea infuser or strain the tea to discard the spent flowers.
Drink the tea while it is still warm, up to 3 times a day, for its potential anti-inflammatory and antiviral effects on viral hepatitis.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St. John's wort tea helps with viral hepatitis because it contains antiviral properties that can potentially inhibit the replication of the hepatitis virus in the body. The tea is made from the flowers, leaves, and stem of the St. John's wort plant, which contains several active compounds, including hypericin and hyperforin. These compounds have been shown to have antiviral, anti-inflammatory, and immunomodulatory effects, which can help reduce liver inflammation, improve liver function, and enhance the body's overall response to the virus. Moreover, St. John's wort tea can help boost the immune system, which is crucial in fighting off the virus and preventing its spread in the body. However, it is important to note that St. John's wort tea should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment or medication prescribed by a healthcare professional for viral hepatitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: St. John's wort tea can reduce inflammation in the liver, which is important for viral hepatitis as it can help alleviate symptoms and promote liver health. The active constituents responsible for this property include hypericin and hyperforin.

Antiviral: St. John's wort tea has been shown to have antiviral properties, which can help inhibit the replication of the hepatitis virus. The active constituents responsible for this property include hypericin and hyperforin.

Antioxidant: St. John's wort tea contains antioxidants that can help protect liver cells from damage caused by free radicals. This property is important for viral hepatitis as it can help prevent further liver damage. The active constituents responsible for this property include hypericin, hyperforin, and flavonoids.

Hepatoprotective: St. John's wort tea has hepatoprotective properties, which means it can help protect the liver from damage. This property is important for viral hepatitis as it can help support liver function and promote recovery. The active constituents responsible for this property include hypericin, hyperforin, and flavonoids.

Immune-boosting: St. John's wort tea can help boost the immune system, which is important for viral hepatitis as it can help the body fight off the virus. The active constituents responsible for this property include hypericin, hyperforin, and flavonoids.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of St. John's wort are rich in hypericin and hyperforin, which have been shown to have antiviral properties, making them useful for treating viral hepatitis.
Leaves: The leaves of St. John's wort contain high levels of hypericin and flavonoids, which have been shown to have hepatoprotective effects, making them a good choice for medicinal tea.
Stems: The stems of St. John's wort contain hypericin and other compounds that have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, making them useful for treating viral hepatitis.

Rhizomes: The rhizomes of St. John's wort contain hypericin, hyperforin, and other compounds that have been shown to have hepatoprotective and antiviral effects, making them a valuable addition to medicinal tea.
remedy_recipe: Measure 2 teaspoons of dried St. John's Wort herb.
Place the herb in a tea infuser or teapot with a strainer.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the herb and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Remove the tea infuser or strain the tea into a cup.
Drink the tea, 2-3 times a day, for a maximum of 2 weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese foxglove tea helps with viral hepatitis because it contains antiviral properties that can help to inhibit the replication of the hepatitis virus in the body. The tea is made from the dried leaves of the Chinese foxglove plant, which has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries to treat various liver ailments. The antioxidants present in the tea can help to reduce inflammation and protect liver cells from damage caused by the virus. Additionally, Chinese foxglove tea can help to improve liver function and promote the healing process, making it a valuable addition to a treatment plan for individuals with viral hepatitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Reduces inflammation in the liver, which is important for viral hepatitis as it can help alleviate symptoms and promote liver healing. This property is attributed to the triterpenoid saponins present in Chinese foxglove tea.

Antiviral: Inhibits the replication of viruses, including hepatitis viruses, which is crucial for managing viral hepatitis. The antiviral properties are attributed to the presence of alkaloids and flavonoids in Chinese foxglove tea.

Hepatoprotective: Protects liver cells from damage, which is important for viral hepatitis as it can help prevent further liver damage. This property is attributed to the presence of steroidal glycosides in Chinese foxglove tea.

Immunomodulatory: Modulates the immune system, which is important for viral hepatitis as it can help regulate the body's response to the virus. This property is attributed to the presence of polysaccharides in Chinese foxglove tea.

Antioxidant: Reduces oxidative stress in the liver, which is important for viral hepatitis as it can help prevent further liver damage. This property is attributed to the presence of phenolic acids and flavonoids in Chinese foxglove tea.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain active compounds that have antiviral properties, making them effective in treating viral hepatitis.
Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which can help reduce liver inflammation caused by viral hepatitis.
Flowers: Contain flavonoids and other compounds that have been shown to protect the liver and support its function during viral hepatitis.
Seeds: High in essential fatty acids and antioxidants, which can help reduce oxidative stress and support liver health during hepatitis.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 2 cups of water and 3 tablespoons of dried Chinese foxglove (Rehmannia glutinosa) root.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 30 minutes.
Add 1 tablespoon of dried licorice root and 1 tablespoon of dried milk thistle seeds to the pot.
Simmer the tea for an additional 15 minutes, then strain and discard the solids.
Drink the tea while it is still warm, up to three times a day between meals.
